113768.explanation: It is about quality. We go for the best person regardless of the candidate’s gender.

113769.explanation: We agree with a referendum under conditions, but only in the preliminary phase of major decisions, not corrective and only advisory.

113770.explanation: If there is demand from the neighborhood, it should be possible. However, good agreements and building technical inspection must be arranged in advance. Residents must not be saddled with unforeseen costs.

113772.explanation: The center of Enschede is the city’s showcase and offers entertainment through its leading events and cultural offerings. If entrepreneurs jointly want longer opening hours, we will respond to that.

113775.explanation: Enschede has had an asylum seeker center for some time without any disturbances. The current asylum reception is made possible under strict conditions. In case of nuisance, strict measures will be taken, which can lead to closure.

113776.explanation: We do not want a ban on fireplaces and wood stoves: residents choose for themselves, with respect for their environment.

113778.explanation: Since fireworks will probably no longer be allowed from 2026, the municipality will organize a fireworks or drone show itself.

113790.explanation: Especially in nightlife, problems are growing bigger, more complex, and more dangerous. Due to national budget cuts for the police, more police tasks fall to our community enforcement officers (boa’s). Our boa’s must be given all the tools necessary to perform their duties.

113791.explanation: We support starters taking their first step on the housing market. With a starter loan, we provide perspective on owning a home without jeopardizing healthy municipal finances.

113795.explanation: We believe that everyone can contribute to our society according to their ability. Participation and maintaining rhythm and thus social contacts are important for everyone.

113796.explanation: We advocate phasing out debt forgiveness. Those who truly cannot pay are supported specifically via the poverty budget, so working residents of Enschede are not unnecessarily burdened.

113797.explanation: Currently, an income limit of 120% is already in use and good results are being achieved with this.

113799.explanation: Parking spaces for electric cars must be determined through a neighborhood participation process.

113800.explanation: Paid parking in neighborhoods is only allowed upon request from the residents of that neighborhood themselves. There must be a large majority in the neighborhood.

113801.explanation: It is about the quality of care.

113802.explanation: The food bank supports (young) women in poverty in this regard. If the food bank needs help, we support that.

113803.explanation: The property tax (OZB) may under no circumstances be increased by more than the inflation correction.

113806.explanation: We ensure sufficient space for business growth by both developing new sites and using existing industrial areas more intensively and smartly, so economic opportunities are not lost due to 'no-sale' decisions.

113807.explanation: When allocating rental and owner-occupied homes, Enschede residents with a demonstrable social or economic connection are given priority. Status holders do not automatically receive priority over other housing seekers.

113808.explanation: Nothing is free. Waste processing costs money and must be paid for somehow. Green and plastic waste streams must remain free. We support periodic bulky waste collection rounds in the neighborhoods.

113809.explanation: We have always advocated keeping the swimming pool, but keeping it open has proven unrealistic and there was no political majority from other parties. Meanwhile, a nice alternative is coming in the form of a community center in Glanerbrug.

113810.explanation: Swimming safety is not a luxury but basic safety. Yet, a group of children grows up without sufficient swimming skills. Therefore, we will reintroduce school swimming so that every child learns to swim and is safe in and around water.

113812.explanation: Current coffeeshops may be tolerated if they behave properly; further expansion is not allowed. Use and trade of drugs on the street, and the related nuisance, will not be tolerated.

113813.explanation: To solve the housing shortage in Enschede and allow responsible city growth, substantial and extensive construction is necessary. Flow on the housing market and preventing inappropriate housing are only possible if sufficient homes are available in the middle and higher segments.

113816.explanation: We strengthen neighborhood facilities so they better meet residents’ needs. Every neighborhood and village has a well-accessible community center where social and resident initiatives can take place at affordable rates.

113818.explanation: We want to strengthen accessibility of the city center with new parking facilities, including a parking garage under the bus station for fast access from the north.

113820.explanation: By opening shops on Sunday mornings, we strengthen the local economy and make the city livelier for residents and visitors.

113822.explanation: Especially where nuisance and major littering occur, investments must be made in extra enforcement through camera surveillance and undercover enforcement officers (BOA’s).

113823.explanation: Our green countryside is a core quality of Enschede and must be preserved. It is our landscape, our nature, and an important calling card of the city. Preservation of our countryside is paramount.

113825.explanation: As the largest city at the edge of the Netherlands and in the heart of Europe, an airport is of economic importance. For this, it is important that market parties take over operations and the budget.

113768.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women, that is. In such a government, all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

113769.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Enschede chooses genuine early involvement of residents. Through dialogue, participation and careful consideration, better decisions are made. We see an advisory referendum as a good tool to promote participation.

113770.explanation: If you give people the trust to build something together in their own neighborhood, beautiful things arise. The municipality should not hinder this but rather encourage it.

113772.explanation: Because of public order and safety among other reasons, we want to arrange this together with entrepreneurs and other stakeholders.

113775.explanation: We support the Distribution Act and Enschede must contribute to the reception of people fleeing war and violence. We adhere to the agreements the municipality has made with the national government.

113776.explanation: Due to air pollution and health risks such as heart problems and asthma, we discourage wood stoves. We want to use the Burning Guide (Stookwijzer) for this.

113778.explanation: The organization does not necessarily have to be done by the municipality. New legislation allows residents to establish fireworks associations. Let the municipality collaborate with them for a nice fireworks show on New Year's Eve, in a way and place that minimizes nuisance (for animals).

113790.explanation: Enforcement officers (BOA's) in Enschede have recently been given handcuffs. We think the use of batons or pepper spray belongs to the police. There must be no ambiguity about the roles of BOA's and the police. More police in Enschede is preferred over more equipment for BOA’s.

113791.explanation: Housing is a human right. Yet, in Enschede the number of people who cannot find appropriate housing is growing. For starters, housing has become unaffordable. Many more affordable owner-occupied and rental homes must be built. A starter’s loan can be part of the solution.

113795.explanation: Work and participation are important, but never under coercion. Meaningful work must be meaningful for society but especially for the individual: guidance, training, and suitable work offer real opportunities and perspective. Meaningful participation works only if it is possible, voluntary, and dignified.

113796.explanation: If debt forgiveness of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into big trouble. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support debt forgiveness.

113797.explanation: Too many people with low or middle incomes currently fall outside support schemes. By raising the income threshold, we ensure more residents receive aid and prevent unnecessary poverty or debt.

113799.explanation: More public charging points are needed to make electric driving accessible to everyone. This accelerates the shift to clean transport, improves air quality and ensures that people without their own driveway can also become more sustainable.

113800.explanation: Parking permits can help in areas with high parking pressure but are not an end in themselves. We look at what is needed per neighborhood and combine this with better public transport, more space for bicycles and greenery, and fewer cars in residential areas.

113801.explanation: Good care aligns with who you are. People must be able to choose a care provider that fits their religion, culture or belief, as long as the care remains accessible, equitable and of good quality for everyone.

113802.explanation: Menstrual products are not a luxury but a basic need. By providing them free of charge, we combat period poverty and ensure everyone can fully participate at school, work and in society.

113803.explanation: We choose fair distribution of burdens, where those who can bear the most carry the heaviest loads. The property tax (OZB) is needed to invest in housing, care, poverty reduction and sustainability, precisely for those who need it most.

113806.explanation: We choose quality over quantity. First, we utilize and make existing business parks more sustainable (for example the harbor area) and combine living, working and greenery. In this way, we protect the countryside and ensure a sustainable economy in Enschede.

113807.explanation: Everyone has the right to a fair chance at housing. We do ensure fast, humane housing of status holders, distributed across the city without displacing other housing seekers.

113808.explanation: Litter in the streets is annoying, unsafe and unhealthy. It is much better if people bring their waste so the municipality can process it properly. Except for residual waste, we believe the recycling center should be free for private individuals.

113809.explanation: Swimming is important, but a new swimming pool requires a lot of money and space. We focus on good maintenance, accessibility, and distribution of existing pools. We'd rather invest that money in safe cycling routes, public transport, and facilities that the residents of Glanerbrug truly benefit from.

113810.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country and every child should learn to swim. Including children whose parents cannot afford swimming lessons. Unfortunately, this is still not fairly arranged. The best solution: school swimming for every child, with support from the municipality.

113812.explanation: A properly regulated coffeeshop policy is part of a safe and livable city. Allowing new coffeeshops under clear conditions helps spread nuisance, increases control, and improves public health.

113813.explanation: Especially in Enschede there is a great need for affordable housing. We want at least 40% social rental housing in new developments, so that starters, young people, and low-income individuals have a fair chance at suitable housing.

113816.explanation: Community centers are important for meeting, welfare, and cohesion. Every neighborhood should have an accessible place where residents can meet each other, organize activities, and have support close by.

113818.explanation: We want fewer cars in the city center and more space for pedestrians, cyclists, greenery, and places to stay. Parking will be managed through park-and-ride facilities at the city outskirts and good public transport.

113820.explanation: We support free/flexible opening hours. If shopkeepers and customers want the store open, it should be possible. This makes it much easier for single people who work during the week to do important shopping.

113822.explanation: We are cautious about this. Litter must be addressed, but camera surveillance impacts privacy. We first opt for better design, education, and enforcement. Only if really necessary and done carefully can camera surveillance be a last resort.

113823.explanation: Nature areas are valuable and vulnerable and must be protected. We will meet the housing demand within the existing city through reuse, densification, and smart integration, not by sacrificing nature.

113825.explanation: Twente Airport costs a lot of public money and delivers too little. We would rather invest that money in housing, public transport, sustainability, and facilities that the residents of Enschede truly benefit from.

113768.explanation: The VVD believes that mayors and aldermen should be appointed based on quality, not on a fixed quota. A mandatory equal division between men and women might lead to not choosing the best candidate.

113769.explanation: Referendums often provide a simplified yes/no answer to complex issues and can raise expectations that are not feasible. We prefer to involve residents through conversations, consultation, and participation so that ideas and concerns are properly considered in decisions.

113770.explanation: The VVD sees it positively if residents are given more room to take responsibility for their living environment. When residents or associations want to manage, for example, a sports hall or a park, this can lead to more involvement and more efficient management.

113772.explanation: The VVD believes entrepreneurs themselves know best when to open. Bars and restaurants know what works for their guests and staff.

113775.explanation: At present, we have agreements with the minister to host a maximum of 550 asylum seekers in Enschede, which is less than required by the distribution law. If the minister breaks this agreement, we want to close asylum reception as soon as possible.

113776.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves are part of the home comfort for many people. A general ban goes too far and is hard to enforce. We'd rather focus on responsible use and limiting nuisance than imposing new bans.

113778.explanation: The VVD believes a municipal fireworks show on New Year's Eve can contribute to a festive and safe celebration. A central fireworks display reduces nuisance and can discourage the use of illegal and dangerous fireworks.

113790.explanation: Enforcement officers increasingly face aggression. Carrying a baton contributes to their own safety and authority on the street.

113791.explanation: Starters find it difficult to buy a home, but a starter loan drives up prices. Additional borrowing capacity increases demand while supply remains limited, making houses more expensive. We focus fully on building homes for all target groups.

113795.explanation: Those receiving social assistance benefits who are able can contribute through socially meaningful work, gain work experience, and build a daily routine. This helps people take steps towards paid employment and increases their independence.

113796.explanation: Fully waiving municipal taxes removes the incentive for some people to take steps toward work and independence. We prefer customized support that activates.

113797.explanation: Working more should be rewarding. Raising the income limit makes working more (or more hours) less attractive.

113799.explanation: The parking pressure in Enschede is high. We believe charging points for electric cars should not come at the expense of regular parking spaces.

113800.explanation: The VVD is against introducing parking permits in more neighborhoods. Parking permits impose additional rules and costs on residents and do not always solve parking problems.

113801.explanation: The VVD believes good and accessible care for everyone should come first. In practice, it is not always possible to fully tailor care to someone's religion, culture, or beliefs, especially given current staff shortages.

113802.explanation: For people with financial difficulties, there are already arrangements and support via poverty policy. Providing tampons and sanitary pads for free to everyone is not a core municipal task.

113803.explanation: The property tax is a fixed and tangible burden for many residents and entrepreneurs. By lowering property tax, people keep more money and we strengthen the business climate. The municipality should critically review its expenses instead of raising taxes.

113806.explanation: The VVD believes Enschede should provide room for entrepreneurs who want to grow and establish themselves. Currently, companies leave Enschede because there is not enough space. More space for business parks is needed to retain employment and attract new jobs.

113807.explanation: Priority for one specific group increases feelings of inequality and is unfair to residents who have often waited years for housing. We focus mainly on building more homes so that the pressure on the social rental market is structurally relieved.

113808.explanation: The VVD supports free disposal of waste at the recycling center. By making it easy and free for residents to dispose of waste properly, we reduce the chance of illegal dumping and additional placing. This ensures a cleaner city.

113809.explanation: The municipality is currently building a new swimming pool at the site of the Aquadrome, which will be accessible to all residents of Enschede. Building and maintaining an additional pool in Glanerbrug would involve too high costs.

113810.explanation: The VVD values children learning to swim well but does not see school swimming as a municipal task. Parents are primarily responsible for swimming lessons, and arrangements exist for families who find this financially difficult.

113812.explanation: The VVD opposes allowing new coffeeshops. Additional coffeeshops increase the risk of nuisance and crime. Given Enschede's location near Germany, we also want to prevent drug tourism. The current coffeeshops are sufficient.

113813.explanation: The VVD thinks it is important that the housing stock in Enschede is balanced. With 36% social rental housing already, that share is high. We want more room for mid-rent and owner-occupied homes. That promotes housing flow and creates a mixed city where different income groups can live comfortably.

113816.explanation: Meeting places in neighborhoods are important. Needs vary per neighborhood, and sometimes existing facilities are sufficient or cooperation with other locations is more logical. Therefore, we take a pragmatic approach.

113818.explanation: Additional parking spaces help improve accessibility and prevent cruising and nuisance in surrounding neighborhoods. More parking also enables more visitors, which is important for a lively city center.

113820.explanation: Entrepreneurs should decide for themselves when to open. More freedom increases the attractiveness of the city center and fits with a modern city. Participation remains voluntary: no one is obliged to open.

113822.explanation: At underground waste containers, waste is often illegally added, which cannot continue. Camera surveillance helps to tackle offenders and prevents illegal placing. Offenders, in our view, should receive fines of 1,000 euros, just like in Singapore.

113823.explanation: The housing shortage is large and requires realistic choices. By compensating nature and imposing quality requirements, housing and nature can go hand in hand.

113825.explanation: The airport is of strategic importance for the region, including for innovation and employment. However, investments must be critically assessed: it must truly benefit us.

113768.explanation: D66 considers diversity in city governance very important. There must be attention to a good balance between people from different backgrounds, not only based on gender.

113769.explanation: D66 values input from residents very highly. A referendum can work well for this. Even more important is collaborating with residents before plans are made. That way, residents can truly contribute ideas about the municipality's plans.

113770.explanation: D66 trusts the collective efforts of residents. If residents take over this management, good agreements must be made to ensure that the level of facilities stays up to standard.

113772.explanation: Let entrepreneurs run their businesses. D66 wants entrepreneurs to be able to choose when they want to be open by making joint agreements on this.

113775.explanation: D66 wants long-term agreements about the asylum seekers center in Enschede. No uncertainties and no renegotiating every year about the necessary aid for people fleeing war and violence. The starting points are our 'fair share', a good reflection of the population, and clear agreements with the COA (Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers).

113776.explanation: Wood burning is unhealthy for residents. That is why D66 supports a ban on soot chimneys in new construction. Additionally, we want Enschede to join the Stookwijzer (Burning Guide).

113778.explanation: D66 is in favor of a complete fireworks ban. Instead, the municipality should organize central fireworks displays.

113790.explanation: For D66, safety starts with preventing and de-escalating unsafe situations and enforcing rules where necessary. A baton helps enforcement officers to do this more safely and effectively.

113791.explanation: The cost of the housing crisis mainly falls on first-time home buyers. That is why D66 wants to invest in this group, especially by realizing 1,000 temporary homes, but also through specific support.

113795.explanation: A social assistance benefit is a last safety net.

113796.explanation: The municipality must help people in financial trouble. This ensures they can contribute positively to society faster. Waiving municipal taxes helps with this.

113797.explanation: It is especially important that people can get help to prevent falling into debt.

113799.explanation: D66 chooses sustainable and clean transport. Electric cars contribute to a sustainable future and therefore D66 wants to support this.

113800.explanation: Recently, the parking permit area has been expanded to the entire inner ring area. When parking pressure in a neighborhood becomes high, D66 prefers to focus on improving alternative transport.

113801.explanation: Diversity is important in all professions, including aid workers. D66 trusts in the professionalism of recognized aid workers, regardless of their cultural or religious background.

113802.explanation: D66 supports combating period poverty. Providing free menstrual products in municipal buildings helps with this.

113803.explanation: Levying taxes is not a hobby for D66, but lowering property tax (OZB) also means cutting back on facilities in the city. We do not want that.

113806.explanation: Enschede currently faces a shortage of space for businesses. For future employment, it is important that we provide that space.

113807.explanation: Prioritizing refugees with residence permits is unnecessary if municipalities, including Enschede, adhere to national agreements for housing status holders.

113808.explanation: D66 wants to increase accessibility of the recycling center by extending opening hours. D66 wants the first 75kg to remain free.

113809.explanation: D66 supports swimming water in all city districts. This can be realized by the municipality but D66 also welcomes it being realized by other parties.

113810.explanation: It is vital that children learn to swim well. However, the best way according to D66 is to attend regular swimming lessons. The municipality should help parents who cannot afford this, so all children can learn to swim.

113812.explanation: D66 supports legalizing soft drugs. D66 has also fought for Enschede to participate in the national pilot. However, we have also seen that coffeeshops caused much nuisance. Currently, a good balance has been found.

113813.explanation: It is important to build many new homes, both affordable and more expensive. But looking at demographic developments, it is especially important to focus on the movement of older people through the housing market.

113816.explanation: It is important that people can meet each other. This should be possible in every neighborhood. This does not always have to be in its own building but can also be a sports canteen that is hardly used during the week.

113818.explanation: D66 chooses parking on the edge of the city instead of in the center. Where this is not yet the case, D66 wants parking hubs with public transport or shared bike connections to the city center.

113820.explanation: Let entrepreneurs run their businesses. D66 wants entrepreneurs to be able to choose when they want to be open by expanding opening hours.

113822.explanation: D66 is only in favor of cameras if their use is proportional and effective. We also highly value the privacy of our residents. Use near waste containers seems promising to us, but we advocate continuous evaluations of the effectiveness of this measure.

113823.explanation: D66 wants new homes to be built primarily within the city. This way, we preserve our beautiful countryside.

113825.explanation: The airport is a special place: a historic airport site that has grown into a breeding ground for innovation, technology, and safety. D66 sees the future of Twente Airport not in more flights but in more value for Twente.

113768.explanation: The CDA chooses the best available leaders based on quality, knowledge of dossiers, and support. Diversity is important but not a strict quota.

113769.explanation: The CDA wants to listen before deciding, with area- and neighborhood-oriented work. A referendum is often too black-and-white for complex issues.

113770.explanation: This fits with shared responsibility: residents and associations that can do better are given space. The municipality facilitates where possible.

113772.explanation: The CDA opts for fixed opening hours to ensure safety around nightlife. Coordinated closing times make supervision, enforcement, and deployment of police and enforcement officers more effective and prevent peak crowds, unsafe situations, and nuisance in the city center.

113775.explanation: Enschede takes responsibility for asylum reception, provided it is manageable, safe, and focused on rapid integration. Clear agreements have been made with the COA about capacity, safety, and the duration of reception.

113776.explanation: No total ban; but action against demonstrable nuisance and focus on cleaner alternatives.

113778.explanation: With national developments moving toward a consumer ban, a central fireworks show can be a safer and better manageable alternative. Additionally, modern variants such as a drone or laser show can be chosen.

113790.explanation: The CDA believes that enforcement officers should have appropriate means to perform their work safely.

113791.explanation: Start-up loans lower barriers in the housing market for young people and young families.

113795.explanation: The CDA finds it important that people on social assistance participate in society to the best of their ability. Socially meaningful work can contribute to structure, self-worth, and involvement, provided there is appropriate guidance and personal circumstances are taken into account.

113796.explanation: The CDA believes that exemption from municipal taxes remains necessary to prevent residents with a (too) low income from getting into further financial trouble. Guaranteeing livelihood security and preventing debts are paramount in this.

113797.explanation: The CDA recognizes that residents with an income just above the current thresholds can also experience financial pressure. By raising the income limit, the municipality can better detect and prevent hidden poverty. Guaranteeing livelihood security and preventing debts remain the priority.

113799.explanation: The CDA considers the expansion of charging infrastructure necessary to enable the transition to sustainable mobility. A balanced distribution across neighborhoods and villages is important, with attention to the available public space.

113800.explanation: Parking problems require customized solutions and an integrated assessment, which also considers accessibility, facilities, and the position of residents and visitors. A generic expansion of permit zones is therefore not desirable.

113801.explanation: The CDA values personalized care and believes it is important that people feel seen and understood. Freedom of choice contributes to trust and quality of care, within the applicable legal and quality frameworks.

113802.explanation: The CDA sees this as part of a dignified and preventive poverty policy. Access to basic products prevents exclusion and supports equal opportunities, especially for young people and vulnerable groups.

113803.explanation: The CDA strives for restraint in local levies. A reduction of the property tax (ozb) is desirable where financially responsible, while maintaining a solid municipal budget.

113806.explanation: The CDA first opts to make better use of existing business parks, restructure and make them more sustainable before considering spatial expansion.

113807.explanation: The CDA values equal and explainable rules for housing seekers. Priority based on status undermines support for housing policy.

113808.explanation: The CDA believes that making recycling centers completely free is not an obvious measure. Waste policy requires a careful balance between affordability, personal responsibility, and efficient use of facilities. Preventing illegal dumping mainly calls for targeted enforcement.

113809.explanation: The CDA understands the desire in Glanerbrug for accessible swimming facilities. At the same time, recent investments have been made in new swimming amenities at the Diekman in Enschede. Basic facilities should always be evenly distributed between city and village.

113810.explanation: The CDA sees the importance of swimming skills but believes that municipal funds should primarily be used to support families in poverty. Through targeted support, parents can make their own choices for swimming lessons that fit their situation.

113812.explanation: The CDA sees no need to allow an expansion of the current number of coffeeshops or to change coffeeshop policy. The focus lies on manageability, safety, and preventing additional nuisance.

113813.explanation: The CDA wants more balance in the housing supply so that first-time buyers, families, and movers have prospects for a suitable home.

113816.explanation: The CDA sees community centers as important places for meeting, support, and volunteer work. However, tailored approaches per neighborhood are needed, matching demand and scale.

113818.explanation: The CDA believes that the limited space in the city center must be allocated carefully. Additional parking spaces often come at the expense of housing, greenery, and quality of stay. We strive for a broad mix of accessibility and parking facilities.

113820.explanation: The CDA values quiet moments and freedom of choice for entrepreneurs. Shopkeepers should not be forced by competitive pressure to extend opening hours constantly.

113822.explanation: Camera surveillance can be an effective tool in reducing illegal dumping and improving enforcement, provided it is used carefully and purposefully.

113823.explanation: The CDA prioritizes the protection of nature and landscape and primarily chooses urban solutions and redevelopment when building homes.

113825.explanation: The CDA believes Twente Airport can continue to play a role in the economic development and accessibility of the region. Investments must be careful, transparent, and limited, with clear agreements about risks, societal added value, and financial responsibility.

113768.explanation: The Christian Union Enschede finds it important that the municipal executive seeks a capable and diverse board. However, strict demands on diversity can also have undesirable effects.

113769.explanation: Referendums are not a solution for complex political issues, as they often create false expectations. We are glad that advisory referendums have been withdrawn recently. The municipal council remains ultimately responsible for political decisions.

113770.explanation: The Christian Union supports initiatives where society takes responsibility itself. However, the municipality ultimately remains responsible should problems arise.

113772.explanation: The Christian Union believes the municipality must maintain control here, for example, to prevent nuisance.

113775.explanation: The Christian Union believes that Enschede must continue to take responsibility in the national challenge of refugee reception.

113776.explanation: The Christian Union supports freedom in this area because sometimes, due to financial constraints, this method of heating is chosen. Moreover, a ban is unenforceable.

113778.explanation: If a national fireworks ban is imposed, the Christian Union finds it logical to organize a municipal alternative. This will probably pay off with less nuisance from illegal fireworks.

113790.explanation: The violence that enforcement officers (boa's) sometimes face justifies them being equipped with a baton to defend themselves.

113791.explanation: The Christian Union supports people trying to buy their first home who encounter high thresholds. A starter loan is a good tool to assist this group.

113795.explanation: We are not in favor of mandatory volunteer work tied to social assistance benefits. However, it may be a tool to regain a work rhythm and better prepare for the labor market. Tailored approaches are important here.

113796.explanation: This is very important to many residents with low income. Therefore, the municipality must continue to provide this.

113797.explanation: The Christian Union advocates broad poverty reduction. Currently, many people still fall between the cracks. We need to explore how to cover this within the available financial space.

113799.explanation: The municipality must respond to needs in the neighborhood. It is important that there are sufficient charging points, but if there are hardly any electric cars in a neighborhood, sacrificing scarce parking spaces is irrational.

113800.explanation: We support the current policy where the neighborhood can indicate whether there is a need for paid parking and parking permits.

113801.explanation: Identity-based care must remain accessible in the municipality of Enschede. When care cannot be provided via a Care-in-Kind contract, a personal budget can be an instrument to request appropriate care under the direction of the care recipient.

113802.explanation: Not in general. However, this must certainly be accessible to people on minimum income, for example via the food bank. We are pleased with the menstrual product distribution points established in Enschede on the initiative of our residents.

113803.explanation: Unfortunately, the municipality does not have the financial position to make this choice. To do so, we would need to make extreme cuts, which the Christian Union finds undesirable.

113806.explanation: There is a shortage of business parks in Enschede. However, the Christian Union believes we should maximize the optimal use of existing sites as much as possible.

113807.explanation: We must continue to provide housing for status holders as required by our legal duty.

113808.explanation: This costs a lot of money that everyone will have to pay via the waste tax. We find it fairer that those who dispose of the waste pay. We consider the 75 kg exemption a good rule to keep.

113809.explanation: It has become clear in the past period that there was no conclusive business case for a swimming pool in Glanerbrug. Unfortunately, the municipality does not have the financial capacity to realize this.

113810.explanation: We will maintain the current safety net scheme so that everyone has the opportunity to obtain a swimming diploma.

113812.explanation: The Christian Union supports the closure of all coffeeshops. Drug addictions unfortunately cause a lot of damage in our communities. And the normalization of drug use through coffeeshops is, in our view, a negative development.

113813.explanation: The Christian Union supports building in all segments. Fortunately, Enschede comfortably meets the national standard of 30%. However, we do not want housing corporations to be prohibited from building when they are able to due to an artificial limit.

113816.explanation: Unfortunately, many people in Enschede suffer from loneliness. Community centers provide space for meeting and social initiatives. This helps connect people and combat loneliness.

113818.explanation: There are many plans for housing construction within the city center. The number of homes will also increase, which requires sufficient parking facilities, preferably in parking garages.

113820.explanation: The Christian Union supports a pause for society, allowing people to catch their breath, and therefore does not support extending Sunday opening hours for shops.

113822.explanation: Camera surveillance can help reduce nuisance around underground waste containers. However, the Christian Union believes this should be used purposefully and not routinely.

113823.explanation: The Christian Union believes our rural areas are something to cherish. We want to protect these areas as much as possible during new construction. Nature reserves are even more important to preserve.

113825.explanation: Enschede should not invest additional money in Twente Airport. The current investments may continue, according to the Christian Union.

113768.explanation: At this moment, the executive board consists only of men. Volt has advocated for diversity in the board over the past four years. We believe the next board of Mayor and Aldermen should be a good reflection of society.

113769.explanation: It is important to bring more democracy to decisions, but the referendum is not suitable for this. Complex issues are reduced to a yes-no question. Volt favors new initiatives such as a citizens' assembly where residents give binding advice to the municipal council.

113770.explanation: There are already cases in Enschede where residents have taken over management of, for example, parks. Volt sees this as very useful. The possibility must certainly exist, but in some cases the municipality is better off managing.

113772.explanation: Volt considers a lively city center important. Entrepreneurs already have great freedom in what they may do. However, nuisance must be limited. Cafés and restaurants should be able to decide their opening and closing times within set frameworks.

113775.explanation: Enschede also contributes to solving the reception crisis. Volt voted in favor of the AZC at Parkweg. The AZC at Parkweg does not cause nuisance; closing it would only lead to problems elsewhere in the country.

113776.explanation: Volt wants, following Apeldoorn’s example, a local regulation that keeps wood burning in check. For instance, burning would not be allowed when there is no wind. This is to limit nuisance from fireplaces and wood stoves for people with asthma and COPD.

113778.explanation: Now that fireworks will no longer be allowed next year, a replacement fireworks show is a great moment to come together and organize the fireworks professionally.

113790.explanation: BOAs’ equipment should match the tasks they perform. BOAs do not actively engage in dangerous situations and should leave such situations to the police. Volt believes carrying weapons like batons is unnecessary as it provokes aggression and increases the likelihood of use.

113791.explanation: Housing is a basic necessity, but opportunities to get a home are unevenly distributed. Many starters struggle to get enough mortgage. Some municipalities experiment with a small extra mortgage for starters via the municipality, leading to a fairer playing field.

113795.explanation: People do not just end up on welfare. Forcing work causes extra stress and is often not beneficial in the long term. The municipality can encourage (volunteer) work.

113796.explanation: Volt wants everyone to make ends meet with their income. Waiving municipal taxes helps with this, allowing residents to have more left for fixed costs. If this is abolished, people will only get into bigger trouble.

113797.explanation: Volt wants less poverty. Given the high daily costs and poverty in Enschede, the threshold could certainly be raised. But it must also be financially feasible. Furthermore, Volt does not see raising the poverty line as a lasting solution to poverty.

113799.explanation: Driving electric is an important part of the energy transition, but not yet accessible to everyone. Therefore, more public charging points must be established, well distributed across neighborhoods, so there are enough facilities for people who cannot charge their car at home.

113800.explanation: In consultation with residents, we will introduce parking permits and paid parking on streets in more neighborhoods around the center to discourage car use and encourage parking in garages. This will make the city more accessible for pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport users.

113801.explanation: Due to aging, there are major shortages in healthcare making this unfeasible. The municipality does not determine healthcare organizations’ policies and should not want to. The municipal healthcare obligation includes providing support that matches someone's care needs, not their beliefs.

113802.explanation: One in eight women in the Netherlands experiences period poverty, meaning they do not have enough money to access menstrual products properly. The municipality can do a lot for them by supporting and supplementing existing initiatives.

113803.explanation: Lowering property tax increases inequality because it significantly reduces the municipality's income, requiring cuts in many areas. This would be a concession to homeowners, who often already have lower fixed costs, at the expense of residents who have a harder time.

113806.explanation: There must be sufficient space for the economy of the future, with industries focused on innovation and sustainability that create jobs for residents. Think of sustainable construction, high-tech, and medical technology. We do not want new industrial estates for warehouses, logistics, and heavy industry.

113807.explanation: Removing the right of way reduces waiting times for other Enschede residents by just one week and does not solve the housing crisis - building more social rental homes does. A fixed place to live ensures that status holders can also find work, allowing them to earn their own money and integrate better.

113808.explanation: Every resident of Enschede can already bring 75 kilos of bulky waste free of charge to the recycling center annually. For most residents, that is more than enough. We will make it easier to dispose of bulky waste with extended opening hours. We help people without a car by collecting waste a few times a year.

113809.explanation: Enschede is already investing in a new swimming pool at the Diekman. Together with the other pools, this provides enough swimming water. Research by the Glanerbrug village council shows that a pool in Glanerbrug is currently too expensive.

113810.explanation: Enschede has many places with water where people who cannot swim well are at risk. Every child in Enschede must therefore have the chance to learn to swim, regardless of whether their parents can afford it.

113812.explanation: There are already many rules regarding coffeeshop locations (for example, not near schools, no longer in the center). A new shop in a neighborhood where rules still allow it should be possible. We do not want to relax the rules because of nuisance and safety concerns.

113813.explanation: Volt wants a minimum percentage of 30% social housing in new construction. A lower percentage seems logical given the current 36%, but in the long term will lead to a shortage of affordable rental homes in new neighborhoods, especially for students and the elderly.

113816.explanation: Volt supports neighborhood and district centers because they foster social cohesion and accessible care. Having a center in every neighborhood and district is very costly. Sometimes it’s better for several neighborhoods to share a center.

113818.explanation: With the current parking garages and other parking options, there are enough places to park cars. Volt wants to encourage bicycle and public transport use to and in the center. Parking spaces for bicycles and shared mobility in the center are important.

113820.explanation: As long as there is no nuisance to residents, Volt is fine with shops choosing to open on Sunday mornings. Longer opening hours mean a more vibrant city.

113822.explanation: Adding more waste unfortunately causes nuisance. Cameras do not prevent waste from being dumped elsewhere. Volt prefers investment in other measures, such as more enforcement and 'green space' around underground containers.

113823.explanation: Volt wants space for nature, walking, and cycling. Nature reserves must remain nature reserves and not become agricultural or residential areas. There are plenty of places within Enschede where building can and must occur without harming nature.

113825.explanation: Volt sees Twente Airport as an important place for (innovative research into) electric and sustainable flying. Financial exploitation can be left to commercial companies, with the municipality making the airport future-proof through a public-private partnership.

113768.explanation: At the PVV we do not discriminate. Men and women are equal in our party and we believe that quality should be the decisive factor when appointing a mayor and/or aldermen.

113769.explanation: The PVV supports citizen participation. Not for nothing have we already made several proposals to achieve this. We will continue to advocate for this.

113770.explanation: The PVV finds it important that residents are actively involved in their living environment and local facilities. At the same time, policy must be realistic and financially responsible.

113772.explanation: Cafés and restaurants should have the freedom to determine their opening hours. However, safety and nuisance for residents must be taken into account.

113775.explanation: The PVV is in favor of a total asylum stop. We should not open a new AZC and close the current one as soon as possible. It is time for Enschede to belong to the people of Enschede again.

113776.explanation: People should decide for themselves how to keep warm. We stop the patronizing.

113778.explanation: People should still be allowed to set off fireworks themselves. A municipal fireworks show replacing a long-standing tradition is unacceptable to the PVV Enschede.

113790.explanation: This contributes to a safer city.

113791.explanation: The PVV supports enabling municipal starter loans for people buying a house for the first time, because this helps them gain access to the owner-occupied housing market, combined with striving for more construction, lower thresholds, and maintaining tax benefits for buyers.

113795.explanation: The PVV believes that, where possible, people on social assistance benefits should perform socially valuable work as a counter-performance to promote involvement.

113796.explanation: Life is already unaffordable for most people in Enschede. It is precisely good that the municipality offers a helping hand to those who need it.

113797.explanation: The insane price increases of recent years have caused even people with average incomes to struggle to make ends meet. Many more people could use help.

113799.explanation: The municipality should stay away from this topic. People who purchase an electric car should arrange their own charging infrastructure. No tax money should be spent on this.

113800.explanation: The PVV is against expanding parking permits to more neighborhoods in Enschede, because this imposes extra costs on residents without structurally solving parking problems.

113801.explanation: Healthcare in the Netherlands must be free from religion, culture, and worldview.

113802.explanation: The PVV opposes the free distribution of tampons and sanitary pads by the municipality, because this is not a core government task and support should be targeted at genuine need through existing aid agencies.

113803.explanation: The PVV advocates lowering the property tax, so that housing costs for residents and expenses for entrepreneurs remain manageable and the municipality uses its resources more efficiently.

113806.explanation: The PVV believes Enschede should facilitate more business parks because there is currently a shortage of commercial plots and this is essential for local employment and economic growth, provided this is carefully planned in balance with housing construction and the living environment.

113807.explanation: Priority for status holders is unacceptable. Our own people are harshly discriminated against by this and must live at home until their forties.

113808.explanation: The PVV supports making waste disposal free at the environmental street, because residents already pay waste disposal taxes and separating waste should not be additionally penalized.

113809.explanation: It is time to do something for the people of Glanerbrug. The previous College brutally lied to the people of Glanerbrug and demolished the swimming pool.

113810.explanation: The PVV supports reinstating and supporting school swimming so every child can learn to swim and would welcome local financial investment in swim education and sports participation as part of sports and health.

113812.explanation: The PVV is strongly against the establishment of new coffeeshops, because these demonstrably cause more nuisance, drug crime, and further deterioration of safety and livability in neighborhoods.

113813.explanation: The PVV finds a share of 35 to 40 percent social housing in Enschede acceptable and therefore sees no need to further increase this share in new construction.

113816.explanation: The PVV is generally in favor of community centers if they contribute to cohesion and livability in the neighborhood but is critical of structural subsidies, abuse, and activities not in the interest of local residents.

113818.explanation: The PVV supports more parking spaces in the city center, because good accessibility is essential for residents, entrepreneurs, and a lively inner city.

113820.explanation: The PVV believes shops should also be allowed to open on Sunday mornings, so entrepreneurs can decide what is best for their business and residents have more freedom and choice.

113822.explanation: The PVV favors more camera surveillance and therefore supports camera surveillance at underground waste containers to tackle nuisance and illegal dumping and to keep public spaces clean and safe.

113823.explanation: The PVV opposes new construction in nature areas, because nature must remain protected and housing construction should be solved within existing buildings and urbanized areas.

113825.explanation: The PVV wants the airport to remain open due to its importance for the regional economy and accessibility and because of its strategic role in safety, emergencies, and defense.

113768.explanation: A more balanced ratio of men and women in municipal government will lead to better results.

113769.explanation: Direct influence of residents on decisions in their neighborhood through neighborhood tables, citizen panels, and other forms of participation is very important. This way, policy plans are made not only for residents, but with them. This strengthens involvement, transparency, and ownership within the community.

113770.explanation: Ownership and involvement of citizens should be encouraged.

113772.explanation: The municipality sets the framework for opening hours. It is up to cafes and restaurants to decide what suits their audience and situation best within those limits.

113775.explanation: The municipality of Enschede takes responsibility for its share of asylum seeker reception. It is important that this takes place in a humane manner.

113776.explanation: Energy supply and heating of houses are essential. A portion of Enschede residents depends on open fireplaces and wood stoves.

113778.explanation: A municipal fireworks show can contribute to a shared festive atmosphere and can ensure a calmer and safer New Year's Eve.

113790.explanation: When enforcement officers indicate they can no longer properly perform their duties without weapons, it is up to the municipality to make a decision on this.

113791.explanation: It is up to the municipality to contribute to sufficient affordable, quality, and suitable housing for Enschede residents. Providing starter loans is not the municipality's responsibility.

113795.explanation: The municipality of Enschede must apply the human dimension and look together with people at possibilities, impossibilities, wishes, and opportunities.

113796.explanation: Existential security for all Enschede residents is an important task of the municipality.

113797.explanation: The human dimension must be leading. Inflation, energy costs, and more have a great impact on people's existential security. The threshold of 120% has become the new subsistence level. People at or near this threshold must be actively helped by the municipality in consultation and cooperation.

113799.explanation: Depending on the need and necessity, the municipality can consider the added value of charging points for electric cars.

113800.explanation: Where bottlenecks exist, the municipality should work together with residents to look for possible solutions.

113801.explanation: Good healthcare is essential for people in Enschede. Having free choice of suitable caregivers is part of this.

113802.explanation: Combating poverty is a responsibility for all of us. Menstrual poverty is part of this. Support, if needed, for schools, sports clubs, community centers, neighborhood centers, food banks and more can be provided by the municipality of Enschede.

113803.explanation: Housing costs have risen enormously in recent years, with many households spending a disproportionately large part of their income on housing. A lower property tax will help them.

113806.explanation: Enschede has sufficient industrial estates. Many commercial buildings are vacant. Enschede should focus on more affordable housing, of which there is a great shortage.

113807.explanation: The SP agrees with how the allocation of social rental housing is currently arranged in Enschede. It is fair for everyone.

113808.explanation: The municipality can cover the costs through waste disposal charges. By making waste disposal at recycling centers free, we prevent dumping and unnecessary pollution.

113809.explanation: Glanerbrug needs a swimming pool. Promises about this have been made for a long time, Enschede must deliver.

113810.explanation: Swimming is a basic skill that every child in Enschede must master. Therefore, we support parents and schools so that children in Enschede can take swimming lessons, regardless of their financial situation.

113812.explanation: The current coffeeshop policy of tolerance and enforcement should be continued.

113813.explanation: SP Enschede wants at least 50% social housing to be realized in all new construction projects in Enschede. Only in this way do we truly build for the people who have been waiting the longest.

113816.explanation: Every neighborhood, district, and village in Enschede deserves a community center: a place where residents can meet, associations can be housed, and activities can be organized.

113818.explanation: Public space in the city center is not intended for parking spaces. The capacity in parking garages is based on the current demand from residents, visitors, and day-trippers.

113820.explanation: Shops may already be open on Sundays. Retailers who want to open before 12:00 can request an exemption from the municipality.

113822.explanation: Not every resident of Enschede has the means or ability to take bulky waste to disposal sites. SP Enschede wants to improve existing waste collection points in our neighborhoods and villages so residents can neatly offer items, and Twente Milieu can collect them regularly and by appointment.

113823.explanation: New construction should preserve our beautiful Twente nature as much as possible.

113825.explanation: A civilian airport in Twente cannot be profitable; this has been calculated many times. Commercial activities on the airport grounds must be self-sustaining. More flight movements are undesirable due to nearby homes and unique flora and fauna.

113768.explanation: EnschedeAnders supports equality, preferably equal distribution. Suitability and availability can allow for a different distribution.

113769.explanation: A non-binding referendum can help with societal or complex issues. It gives a voice to opposing views instead of excluding them.

113770.explanation: It is a public duty, in principle for the municipality. In consultation, execution can perhaps be done well by residents, while costs and responsibility remain with the municipality.

113772.explanation: Extended opening hours can definitely be discussed. Responsibility and decision-making remain with the municipality. Where possible, fine.

113775.explanation: The presence of this asylum seekers center is temporary and the term has already been agreed upon. Afterwards, reception of foreigners in need must still be possible, including in Enschede. The form this will take must then be considered.

113776.explanation: It is a trade-off of interests. Possibly energy poverty and health: because of energy poverty, open fireplaces and wood stoves are defendable. In the context of health and breathing clean air, they are not.

113778.explanation: If the municipality has to organize it, it will also have to pay for it. An unnecessary and undesirable solution.

113790.explanation: The actions of the enforcement officers should be possible unarmed. In unsafe situations, they should contact the police.

113791.explanation: The municipality must ensure there are sufficient affordable homes.

113795.explanation: 'Must' is too strong a word; if possible, it would be nice. It benefits both residents and society, but it must be feasible.

113796.explanation: Charging fees and waiving them is pointless. Appropriate charging (income-dependent) and incorporating it into the overall financial picture.

113797.explanation: Do not bind to numbers. Look at the support needed and possible solutions; this can apply to every resident.

113799.explanation: The municipality must be facilitating and enable good initiatives. A steering municipality, despite good intentions, will miss the goal.

113800.explanation: Enschede is gradually becoming one large affordable parking lot. Undesirable and certainly does not lead to improved mobility.

113801.explanation: People who need care must be able to receive the care they need. Everyone may refuse, but then there may be no care. Quality and affordability are paramount.

113802.explanation: In public locations, the municipality should make that possible.

113803.explanation: Preferably downwards; the municipality must also balance its budget at appropriate times. The continuous rise in costs for our residents is unsustainable.

113806.explanation: There are sufficient industrial estates; perhaps reshuffling is a good option.

113807.explanation: Giving priority is discriminatory against others.

113808.explanation: Allowing as much free disposal as possible prevents dumping waste in public spaces.

113809.explanation: There has been insufficient swimming water in Enschede for years, including Glanerbrug. Vitality has been taken from young people and (vulnerable) elderly in Glanerbrug; restore that.

113810.explanation: That promotes the health and vitality of our youth. Swimming skills and safety are increased.

113812.explanation: The current number of coffeeshops has been fine for years; keep it that way.

113813.explanation: More affordable homes must be made available for everyone, regardless of labels and regulatory pressure.

113816.explanation: District East, especially Glanerbrug, is severely lagging behind in this area. Something really needs to happen in the coming period!

113818.explanation: There are enough parking spaces in the center; mobility and accessibility are much bigger problems.

113820.explanation: That can perfectly well be left up to the shops themselves whether they want to make use of it or not.

113822.explanation: Temporary use is fine to inventory abuses. Permanent use only makes sense with additional measures. Camera surveillance alone has proven not to be a solution.

113823.explanation: No new construction in vulnerable natural areas.

113825.explanation: Continue investing in a fruitful future. The plan to have companies operate it in the coming years is fine. Keeping it open for military purposes is currently a requirement.

113768.explanation: FVD resolutely rejects identity politics and diversity quotas. Selection should be based solely on individual quality and merit rather than gender, origin, or other external characteristics.

113769.explanation: Introducing binding referenda is an essential spearhead to return political power to the citizen. Direct democracy ensures that residents actually have a say in important decisions in their own environment instead of everything being determined by administrators.

113770.explanation: Strengthening the local community and increasing civic responsibility is an important principle. When residents take responsibility for their public amenities themselves, this promotes social cohesion and careful management of the immediate living environment.

113772.explanation: Entrepreneurs in small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) deserve maximum freedom and must be relieved from excessive bureaucracy. By reducing the excessive regulatory pressure, hospitality entrepreneurs can again decide how to run their business themselves, which benefits local dynamism and self-reliance.

113775.explanation: FVD wants a complete asylum stop and the total dismantling of the COA. The party aims to end asylum accommodation on Dutch territory and focuses on reception in the region to protect national culture, safety, and the housing market.

113776.explanation: FVD strongly opposes government interference in the personal lives of citizens. The party wants residents to retain the freedom to decide how they heat their homes and firmly rejects bans on wood stoves or fireplaces for climate or environmental goals.

113778.explanation: FVD supports preserving the tradition of consumer fireworks and is against fireworks bans. A central show should never be used as a replacement for or an argument to prohibit citizens from setting off fireworks themselves. Citizen freedom is paramount.

113790.explanation: FVD opposes further arming of enforcement officers because safety is a core task of the police. We believe the police must retain the monopoly on violence. Instead of equipping enforcement officers with weapons, FVD prefers to invest in more police officers with full powers and equipment on the street.

113791.explanation: FVD wants to get the housing market moving by building massively and stopping immigration, not through market-distorting loans. We believe such subsidies only push prices higher. We want to abolish transfer tax so that homes truly become affordable.

113795.explanation: FVD believes that benefits are a temporary safety net, not a hammock. We think there should be a counter-performance. By performing socially useful work, people stay involved in society and increase their chances of a full-fledged job in the future.

113796.explanation: FVD wants to drastically reduce the tax burden for everyone, especially for the lowest incomes. We believe the municipality should not first strip citizens bare only to give something back through complicated exemptions. We advocate for lower charges at the base, so people keep more.

113797.explanation: FVD wants to fight poverty by addressing the causes: the exorbitant energy tax and inflation. We believe that raising income limits increases dependence on the government. We rather advocate significant tax relief so that everyone has more to spend themselves.

113799.explanation: FVD opposes the forced energy transition and the favoritism of electric driving. Scarce parking space must not be sacrificed to charging stations. Everyone must have the freedom to drive a car of their choice without government steering.

113800.explanation: FVD opposes treating motorists as 'cash cows.' Parking permits are abused as disguised taxes and restrict freedom of movement. FVD wants to remove restrictions, ensure good accessibility, and make parking free where possible.

113801.explanation: FVD stands for freedom of choice and freedom of education and healthcare. We believe people must not be forced into a state standard. Everyone should be able to receive care that aligns with their own values and beliefs without government ideological interference.

113802.explanation: FVD believes that the government should not provide hygiene products. This is an individual responsibility, not a municipal task. Instead, the party advocates general tax relief so that citizens can pay for these basic needs themselves.

113803.explanation: FVD wants to drastically reduce the burdens for citizens and entrepreneurs. The property tax unnecessarily drives up housing costs; by reducing it, residents of Enschede keep more money, and home ownership is stimulated.

113806.explanation: FVD wants maximum space for entrepreneurs by removing obstructive environmental and nitrogen regulations. More business parks mean jobs, innovation, and a stronger economy in Enschede. The municipality must facilitate, not hinder.

113807.explanation: FVD is strongly against priority for status holders in the housing market. It is unacceptable that residents of Enschede are passed over on waiting lists. The housing shortage must be solved by closing borders and building for the local population rather than asylum seekers.

113808.explanation: FVD considers waste processing a basic service. A free recycling center prevents illegal dumping and reduces burdens. Citizens already pay enough waste disposal tax; extra gate fees are an unjust double tax that we want to abolish immediately.

113809.explanation: FVD wants to drastically cut non-essential government projects and million-euro prestige projects. We believe the market should handle these kinds of facilities. Given the huge municipal debt, Enschede must prioritize lower taxes rather than expensive new construction.

113810.explanation: FVD believes sport and exercise are the responsibility of parents, not the government. We want to drastically cut subsidies for such programs to reduce general burdens. This way, families keep more money to pay for swimming lessons for their children.

113812.explanation: FVD opposes the normalization of drug use and the nuisance caused by coffee shops. We believe the municipality must protect safety and livability in neighborhoods. Instead of more drug sales points, we advocate enforcement and a strong focus on prevention.

113813.explanation: FVD wants to stimulate home ownership and tackle imbalance in the housing market. We believe Enschede already has an above-average amount of social housing. By shifting focus to affordable owner-occupied homes and the private sector, we create more flow-through and strengthen the middle class.

113816.explanation: FVD wants to drastically cut the proliferation of subsidized welfare projects. We believe community centers are often meeting places for bureaucracy and ideology. Let citizens develop initiatives themselves instead of making taxpayers pay for a center in every neighborhood.

113818.explanation: FVD believes the center must remain accessible to everyone. The current hunt on cars must stop. By creating more parking spaces, we attract more visitors to the city, which is essential for the survival of our local retailers and hospitality entrepreneurs.

113820.explanation: FVD stands for maximum freedom of choice for entrepreneurs and citizens. We believe the government should not decide when shopkeepers open their doors. Shopkeepers must be able to decide for themselves whether to open on Sunday morning to serve their customers and strengthen the local economy.

113822.explanation: FVD supports a tough approach to pollution and illegal dumping. We believe camera surveillance is an effective tool to ensure livability and safety in neighborhoods. Those who break the rules and dump waste next to containers must be tackled so that the tidy citizen does not suffer.

113823.explanation: FVD wants to break through environmental rules and nitrogen barriers to address the housing shortage. Building homes for local residents has priority over abstract nature goals. By building responsibly on the city edges, we solve the housing shortage in Enschede.

113825.explanation: FVD sees Twente Airport as a crucial strategic asset. The municipality must invest in its preservation and development to stimulate innovation, business activity, and jobs. Divesting this unique site would, according to FVD, be a major economic blunder.

113768.explanation: The Party for the Animals (PvdD) would actually like to go a step further and have the municipal executive board be a representative reflection of the municipality's residents, not only in terms of the ratio of men and women but also taking education level and origin into account.

113769.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants advisory referenda to have a place in important decisions such as major construction projects or municipal reorganizations. We also enable neighborhood referenda. Residents affected by certain decisions should be able to easily make their voices heard.

113770.explanation: The Party for the Animals encourages residents' initiatives and supports the 'right to challenge,' so that residents can manage municipal facilities if they can do so better.

113772.explanation: Crowding and disturbances caused by people, traffic, noise, and light will disrupt people, animals, and nature in the city. If this continues throughout the night, there is no room for rest and recovery.

113775.explanation: Refugees are welcome. Every municipality should provide a fair share of shelter and focus on small-scale, humane accommodation within the community. Residents of asylum seeker centers are residents of the municipality and, like every other resident, have the right to stability and a good future.

113776.explanation: Wood smoke produces a large amount of fine particulate matter and causes health problems for many residents. Therefore, wood burning should be banned both indoors and outdoors, and as long as this ban is not in place, wood burning should be restricted.

113778.explanation: The Party for the Animals opposes fireworks nuisance. It supports a ban on consumer fireworks and wants no fireworks at events – not even a central show – to protect people and animals. A drone show could be a possible alternative.

113790.explanation: Public enforcement officers (BOAs) are not police and should remain so. They have different training and roles. In case of threat or violence, BOAs should call the police and withdraw. Carrying a baton increases the risk of escalation and can provoke or worsen incidents.

113791.explanation: Providing starter loans is not a municipal task. Moreover, starter loans can lead to higher house prices. Buyers get more buying power and can offer more, causing sellers to raise their asking prices.

113795.explanation: The Party for the Animals puts the resident and their needs at the center and has a broad and generous view of social assistance recipients. This means less coercion and fewer obligations, respecting health problems. A strong emphasis on paid work or a coercive character does not fit here.

113796.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes prevents people from falling into debt and contributes to social security. This also reduces demand for debt counseling and stress-related problems, allowing more funds to be spent on other social initiatives.

113797.explanation: Currently, a part of Enschede’s population lives just above the income limit and receives no assistance, which adversely affects them. Raising this threshold means fewer people experience these negative effects and increases the chances of living a healthy and happy life.

113799.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to accelerate the transition to clean transportation by ensuring sufficient charging points powered by green electricity. Electric cars are more environmentally friendly because they emit no harmful exhaust gases and thus contribute to better air quality and the climate.

113800.explanation: The municipality will introduce paid parking throughout the city and significantly increase the prices of subsequent parking permits. Additionally, there will be variable parking rates and permits based on emissions, weight, and size. This way, cleaner and smaller cars are encouraged and polluting vehicles discouraged.

113801.explanation: Everyone who needs care and support has the right to it, with attention to human scale. Care clients should be able to participate in decisions about their own treatment.

113802.explanation: The Party for the Animals welcomes the free provision of menstrual products but strongly warns that this must not become window-dressing. Without a structural approach to poverty and inequality, it remains mere symptom treatment.

113803.explanation: The Party for the Animals opposes lowering the property tax (OZB). While the national government undermines municipalities financially towards the abyss year 2026, it is irresponsible to forgo local income. Enschede must invest in people, animals, and nature-a reduction of the OZB frustrates that responsibility.

113806.explanation: The Party for the Animals sees that few suitable spaces are available and therefore advocates for densification of existing business parks for the first time. Outdated business parks should be cleaned up and then transformed into residential areas with green space.

113807.explanation: The Party for the Animals views a permanent residence as a condition for optimal integration, taking other housing seekers into account. Flowing through to new housing reduces pressure on asylum seeker centers and contributes to keeping Enschede an open and inclusive city.

113808.explanation: Up to a certain weight, this should be free; beyond that, not. There are costs associated with waste processing, and the polluter must contribute. This also provides an incentive to reduce waste as desired.

113809.explanation: This would be a useful facility but a major cost item, and soon a renovated swimming pool will be available within cycling distance.

113810.explanation: The Party for the Animals Enschede wants the municipality to invest in swimming lessons at schools. It is very important for safety that every child learns to swim.

113812.explanation: The Party for the Animals opposes banning soft drugs. The establishment of legal and regulated coffeeshops should also not be prohibited, except near schools.

113813.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants more affordable housing and therefore wishes that at least 40% of rental homes are social housing.

113816.explanation: Agreed that every neighborhood should have a community center. The municipality invests in youth centers, community centers, neighborhood centers, and meeting places.

113818.explanation: Stationary and moving cars take up a lot of space. Pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport must be central. Where cars currently drive, people can cycle and walk. Where cars are currently parked, greenery can grow and flourish and children can play.

113820.explanation: The Party for the Animals is not in favor of the 24-hour economy and consumerism; on the other hand, we support entrepreneurs’ freedom of choice and spreading activities over time.

113822.explanation: It is important to prevent illegal dumping to keep waste out of the environment. But camera surveillance is ineffective, displaces the problem, and invades privacy.

113823.explanation: Building on green space: no! To keep Enschede livable, we cherish nature areas. Where urban expansion is unavoidable, building on agricultural land is a reasonable alternative.

113825.explanation: Enschede immediately stops compensating annual deficits. The companies benefiting from business flights must finance this themselves.

113768.explanation: PVDR demands active combatting of institutional sexism. We strive for equal representation of women in leadership positions in government. We ensure this through mandatory training and liability for violations of Article 1 of the Constitution.

113769.explanation: PVDR wants to restore democratic control and bridge the gap between citizens and government. We see participation through referenda as an essential tool to protect public values and the rule of law so that power and law serve humanity again.

113770.explanation: PVDR stands for democratic control over public space. We prioritize citizen collectives and cooperatives in managing land and facilities. This restores the bond between citizens and government and makes power serve people again.

113772.explanation: PVDR supports entrepreneurship and wants to stimulate small and medium-sized businesses by giving room for talent and innovation. We advocate for less bureaucracy and a government that supports entrepreneurs rather than unnecessarily limiting them with strict regulations.

113775.explanation: PVDR chooses humanity and justice as universal values. We stand for a society where everyone is protected from injustice, regardless of origin. Asylum policy must be based on humanitarian responsibility and strict adherence to human rights.

113776.explanation: PVDR stands for a sustainable living environment and circular principles. We advocate a course that makes building and living serve both people and the environment again. Limiting harmful emissions fits our goal of a healthy and future-proof rule of law.

113778.explanation: PVDR chooses safety and humanity. A central fireworks show prevents nuisance and injury, fitting our vision of a safe living environment. We want the government to protect public values and bring citizens together in a safe, shared public space.

113790.explanation: PVDR chooses the rule of law, not a police state. We want the government to protect citizens and not fight them out of mistrust. The focus is on human scale and de-escalation; additional weapons for BOAs do not fit our vision of a service-oriented and just government.
