113791.explanation: PVDR sees housing as an enforceable fundamental right. We support measures to help starters, such as affordable energy-neutral homes and active housing market management. Starter loans fit our vision of equal opportunities and a government that serves citizens.

113795.explanation: PVDR stands for a government that protects citizens and does not fight them out of mistrust. We believe benefits must be at a livable level and that citizens must not be crushed by coercive control or system errors. Human dignity is the basis.

113796.explanation: PVDR stands for social security and a strong social safety net. Waiving debts protects vulnerable citizens against institutional injustice and poverty. We want the government to serve people and fairly distribute burdens by taxing big capital more heavily.

113797.explanation: PVDR stands for a strong safety net and believes benefits should be at a livable level. We want the government to serve and protect citizens from injustice. Raising the threshold ensures help is accessible to those who truly need it.

113799.explanation: PVDR advocates sustainable spatial planning with public values at its core. Investing in green infrastructure and facilitating sustainable mobility fits our vision of a future-proof Netherlands where building and living serve both people and the environment.

113800.explanation: PVDR wants to protect citizens from extra burdens and bureaucracy. We often see parking permits as a hidden tax that does not necessarily improve livability. The government must serve citizens and not restrict public space out of mistrust or profit motives.

113801.explanation: PVDR puts the human scale and dignity at the center. Healthcare is a fundamental right and must align with the identity of the citizen. A government that serves must ensure that everyone feels safe and understood in healthcare, regardless of faith or culture.

113802.explanation: PVDR sees access to menstrual care as basic medical care and a fundamental right. We promote the free distribution of sustainable and safe products such as cups and underwear. This enhances human dignity, protects the environment, and combats institutional inequality.

113803.explanation: PVDR wants to reduce the burden on citizens by taxing big capital more heavily. We advocate for a fair distribution where housing is a right, not a cash cow. Lowering property tax (ozb) for residents fits our vision of a government that puts the human scale first.

113806.explanation: PVDR wants to limit the power of big capital on scarce public land. We prioritize public housing and nature over profit-driven business parks. Land must be a public good that serves the human scale and living environment.

113807.explanation: PVDR wants to stop the pitting of groups against each other. We see housing as a fundamental right for everyone. By reserving 40% of new construction for social rental and middle incomes, we solve the shortage for all home seekers without priority damaging social cohesion.

113808.explanation: PVDR stands for a sustainable, circular society in which the government is service-oriented. Free waste collection lowers the barrier for citizens to recycle responsibly and prevents littering or illegal dumping. Public facilities must be accessible to everyone.

113809.explanation: PVDR sees sports and exercise as crucial for public health. Accessible facilities in every community, such as Glanerbrug, guarantee the human scale and social cohesion. We invest in public facilities that are reachable for everyone, regardless of income.

113810.explanation: PVDR regards equal opportunities and safety as core values. School swimming prevents children from low-income families from falling behind. A government that applies the human scale invests in this public basic skill to strengthen the social rule of law.

113812.explanation: PVDR wants to take drugs out of criminality through regulation. This increases safety on the streets and control over quality. A government that applies the human scale pursues a realistic policy based on public health rather than a pointless 'war on drugs'.

113813.explanation: Housing is a fundamental right. PVDR wants at least 40% of new construction to consist of social rental and middle-income homes. Limiting it to 25% worsens the housing shortage and only serves investors’ profits, while we prioritize the human scale and security of existence.

113816.explanation: PVDR wants the government to be service-oriented and promote social cohesion. Neighborhood centers are essential for the human scale and combating loneliness. We prefer to invest in public meeting places rather than in private profit maximization.

113818.explanation: PVDR wants the city to be accessible and welcoming to everyone. By increasing parking capacity, we support local businesses and reduce parking pressure in surrounding residential neighborhoods. We see public space as a facility that must serve citizens, not restrict them.

113820.explanation: PVDR values the human scale and the right to rest. Our economy serves the people, not the other way around. By keeping Sunday mornings free from commerce, we protect workers from constant work pressure and promote social cohesion and reflection in society.

113822.explanation: PVDR rejects mass surveillance and distrust towards citizens. Camera surveillance at waste containers is a disproportionate invasion of privacy. We advocate for a human scale and addressing the cause of illegal dumping instead of constantly monitoring citizens.

113823.explanation: PVDR wants to protect nature and the living environment against the expansion drive of big capital. We solve the housing shortage through active land policy and 40% social/middle construction at existing locations. Nature is a public good we must safeguard for future generations.

113825.explanation: PVDR wants to stop pumping public money into a loss-making airport. We propose an 'out-of-the-box' solution: transform the airport into a self-sufficient student neighborhood. This way we use the land for public housing and the human scale.

113768.explanation: Nonsense. Selection should be based on skills and qualities, not gender.

113769.explanation: Increase involvement, ownership, and trust by giving residents real participation. This increases transparency. Major or sensitive decisions are no longer made behind closed doors alone. What happens in a neighborhood, village, or city is best judged locally by residents themselves.

113770.explanation: If a resident truly wants this and meets all municipal requirements and conditions, why should we stop that? Lower costs for the municipality, more satisfaction among residents.

113772.explanation: Catering entrepreneurs already face a comprehensive package of rules and burdens. They are perfectly capable of deciding what is appropriate for their business themselves.

113775.explanation: Intake must never come at the expense of the safety and quality of life of local residents. Recognizing that the country is full is not a rejection of people but a realistic observation necessary to keep housing, integration, and livability manageable. Local residents must be the priority.

113776.explanation: That this is even a discussion point shows how far we have drifted. Fireplaces and wood stoves provide warmth without relying on gas or electricity grids. It is affordable and offers comfort and freedom of choice for residents.

113778.explanation: The task of the municipality is to guarantee good basic facilities; fireworks are not part of that. A nationwide ban is unenforceable, increases illegal trade, and only makes it more dangerous. And yes: traditions are part of it.

113790.explanation: Those who deploy BOA’s (municipal enforcement officers) for safety must also give them the means and powers to act. A baton without the authority to use it has no value.

113791.explanation: It is intended to bridge the gap between what someone can borrow and what a home costs. The scheme must meet strict conditions, with clear income and purchase price limits. So that it can only be used for hardworking residents, focusing on young people and single earners.

113795.explanation: Those able to provide a socially meaningful counter-performance must also be actively guided towards paid work. Counter-performances should not be a final destination but a step towards regular employment. Social assistance is a temporary safety net, not a hammock. Abuse must be tackled firmly.

113796.explanation: Such arrangements make dependency more attractive and (more) working less rewarding. Debt forgiveness is intended as temporary emergency aid for truly urgent situations, not as structural policy for low incomes. (More) work must pay off again.

113797.explanation: Work must pay off again. Costs in Enschede are already sky-high. Raising income limits undermines the incentive to work. It makes dependency more attractive than working. Dependency is rewarded, effort is not. That is upside down.

113799.explanation: There are already enough charging points in Enschede and options for residents to request new charging stations. Choosing an electric car carries its own responsibility. Further expansion with public money is therefore not necessary.

113800.explanation: The parking policy is already extremely strict and expensive. Introducing parking permits in residential neighborhoods is impractical and unnecessary. The municipality should ensure sufficient parking spaces rather than further restricting parking or clinging at all costs to green strips.

113801.explanation: People who need care should be glad to receive it. Forcing choice based on faith, culture, or beliefs is unacceptable. It would also be unfeasible and result in even longer waiting times.

113802.explanation: Providing free tampons and sanitary pads is not a task of the municipality. The municipality should focus on its core tasks and basic facilities.

113803.explanation: Homeowners are already heavily taxed by the current government and should not be further squeezed by the municipality. The municipality receives structurally too little money from The Hague. More money from the national government would enable tax reductions without putting basic services under pressure.

113806.explanation: In favor of expanding and strengthening business parks to boost the local economy and employment. Entrepreneurs need space to grow. That is essential for a strong and independent city.

113807.explanation: Strongly opposed. Recognizing that the country is full is not a rejection of people but a realistic observation necessary to keep housing, integration, and livability manageable. The PVE stands for priority for local residents in the housing market.

113808.explanation: Municipal taxes in Enschede are already above average. Residents pay a fixed base amount plus variable waste costs. There should be something to show for that. Free disposal at the recycling center encourages correct behavior and prevents nuisance and illegal dumping.

113809.explanation: According to the municipality, reopening the old pool was not financially feasible. They now want to use the location for a neighborhood meeting or multifunctional facility, which residents view as a consolation prize. There is no support for this in the village-wasted tax money.

113810.explanation: This is not a municipal task. Responsibility lies with schools and parents. The municipality does not provide specific, fixed subsidies for school swimming. However, there are general sports subsidies for clubs and financial arrangements via social funds for individual children.

113812.explanation: Provides control, calm, employment, less nuisance on the street and in regular hospitality, and prevents legal disputes. It belongs to the Netherlands. The phase-out policy shifts the problem. Indoor use must remain possible. Legal sales reduce street trade and crime.

113813.explanation: The need for affordable rental and purchase housing is still great; the percentage is just a number, it is reality. More homes for local residents, especially working youth and single earners. So that they have a perspective on independence and a future in their own city.

113816.explanation: Not every neighborhood needs its own community center; almost all neighborhoods already have community and district meeting locations. Tax money can be better spent on priorities that truly affect residents, such as good basic facilities: more affordable housing and quality healthcare.

113818.explanation: More construction is taking place in the city center, so more parking spaces must also be provided. Excluding cars and the so-called greening do not offer a solution for accessibility and are neither realistic nor feasible for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors.

113820.explanation: Entrepreneurs already face a substantial package of rules and burdens. They are perfectly capable of deciding what is appropriate for their own businesses.

113822.explanation: Camera surveillance is a supporting tool for the police and not a solution for waste nuisance. Problems with underground waste containers are the result of failing waste policy. Waste nuisance at underground containers requires better waste management, not extra surveillance.

113823.explanation: Housing construction should first focus on urban areas; there are plenty of opportunities. Think of expansion adjacent to existing buildings, city edges that are not protected nature areas, and redevelopment of outdated business locations, such as the Harbor Area. First utilize what is possible.

113825.explanation: It is owned by the province of Overijssel. The province still needs to make a decision: to rent out to entrepreneurs who take on the operation or to close it. Municipal tax money should not be spent here. First a sound and realistic plan, otherwise no public funds.

113768.explanation: DENK believes it is important that the college of mayor and aldermen reflects society well. Equal opportunities for men and women should be the starting point, with a focus on quality, diversity, and inclusivity.

113769.explanation: DENK Enschede agrees with this. Residents should be able to express their opinions on important issues via a referendum. This strengthens democracy, trust in local politics, increases involvement, and ensures decisions better align with what matters in the city.

113770.explanation: DENK Enschede disagrees. Management of sports halls and public green spaces belongs to the municipality, so that quality, accessibility, and equality for all residents are guaranteed.

113772.explanation: DENK advocates for more trust in the hospitality sector by giving entrepreneurs more control over their own business operations. This boosts the local economy and employment. However, the municipality must maintain a good balance between entrepreneurial freedom and peace in residential areas.

113775.explanation: DENK disagrees. People fleeing danger deserve protection and shelter. Closing asylum centers does not solve problems; good distribution, humane accommodation, and cooperation with residents are necessary.

113776.explanation: DENK Enschede believes residents themselves should have the choice to use fireplaces and wood stoves. At the same time, health and the environment must be considered, for example through conscious use and clear rules, so that comfort and livability go hand in hand.

113778.explanation: At DENK, we believe safety, health, and the environment come first. Instead of fireworks, we prefer safe and sustainable alternatives, such as light or laser shows, so everyone can enjoy without danger or nuisance, and municipal funds are spent on important facilities.

113790.explanation: At DENK, we believe safety mainly concerns good training, presence, and communication, not more armament. By focusing on de-escalation and cooperation with police, enforcement officers, and youth workers, we ensure a safe city without residents feeling threatened.

113791.explanation: DENK is in favor of a starter loan. This gives first-time buyers more chances to purchase a home and helps them overcome hurdles in the housing market.

113795.explanation: We disagree: people receiving social assistance benefits should not be required to perform community work. At DENK, we believe a social assistance benefit is intended to provide everyone with a fair subsistence minimum. Instead of coercion, we focus on guidance.

113796.explanation: At DENK, we believe that people with little need extra support to make ends meet. Tax waivers help them stay financially afloat and contribute to social justice in the city.

113797.explanation: At DENK, we believe more people should have access to support so that no one falls through the cracks. Help must be fair and easily accessible so residents in difficult situations immediately receive the right support.

113799.explanation: At DENK, we believe sustainable transport should be encouraged, so residents can more easily switch to electric vehicles and contribute to a clean and healthy city.

113800.explanation: At DENK, we believe parking should remain accessible and practical for residents and visitors, without extra rules or burdens that complicate daily life.

113801.explanation: At DENK, we believe care must be personal, respectful, and inclusive, so everyone feels safe and understood when receiving help and treatment.

113802.explanation: At DENK, we believe menstrual products are a basic necessity and must not form a financial barrier. Free products ensure equal opportunities, health, and dignity for all residents, regardless of income or background.

113803.explanation: At DENK, we believe it is important that the municipality can properly fund schools, healthcare, and social services, while keeping property tax affordable for both renters and owner-occupiers.

113806.explanation: At DENK, we believe local entrepreneurs should have extra opportunities to grow and offer employment. For large companies and chains, expansion is less necessary; the city may protect its space and livability in that regard.

113807.explanation: DENK stands for equal opportunities in the housing market for everyone. At the same time, we recognize that some groups, such as status holders, face structural inequality they did not cause themselves. Therefore, extra protection is justified.

113808.explanation: At DENK, we believe all residents must have equal access to waste facilities, regardless of income. This encourages correct waste separation, protects the environment, and ensures a clean and livable city for everyone.

113809.explanation: At DENK, we believe sports and exercise must be accessible to all residents. A swimming pool contributes to health, swimming skills, and social encounters, ensuring everyone, young and old, has equal opportunities to play sports and enjoy themselves.

113810.explanation: At DENK, we believe all children must learn to swim, regardless of background or financial situation. Furthermore, sports and exercise must be free for all children so everyone has equal opportunities for a healthy and active youth.

113812.explanation: At DENK, we believe livability and safety in neighborhoods must come first. Instead of expanding coffeeshops, we focus on education, prevention, and support to limit problems around drug use.

113813.explanation: We believe more affordable housing is needed, especially for young people, starters, and families with lower incomes. Social rental homes are essential to give everyone a chance to live in Enschede and to maintain a social, mixed city.

113816.explanation: We agree: every neighborhood in Enschede deserves its own community center. Community centers strengthen social cohesion, provide meeting places, and offer space for activities for young and old. This way we build involved and livable neighborhoods where everyone feels at home.

113818.explanation: We believe extra parking spaces in the city center are desirable only if they contribute to accessibility for residents, the elderly, and entrepreneurs. More parking must not come at the expense of livability, greenery, and safety. First smart solutions and good alternatives.

113820.explanation: We agree: the municipality must enable shops to open on Sunday mornings as well. This gives residents more flexibility to shop, helps workers and families, and supports local entrepreneurs.

113822.explanation: We disagree: there is no need for camera surveillance at underground waste containers. At DENK, we believe residents' privacy is paramount. Problem behavior is not solved with cameras but with social solutions: clear information, neighborhood involvement, and support.

113823.explanation: The municipality may allow new construction in nature areas, but only if done carefully and especially involving social rental housing. At DENK, we believe everyone should have an affordable home and that city growth should not be at the expense of nature.

113825.explanation: Enschede should no longer invest money in Twente Airport. At DENK, we believe public funds are better spent on affordable housing, schools, healthcare, and social services that directly benefit the residents of our city.

113768.explanation: When appointing candidates, the best candidate must always be decisive, regardless of gender.

113769.explanation: Residents must have direct influence on important decisions through active resident consultations and referenda.

113770.explanation: Residents should be able to take over the management of municipal facilities, such as sports halls or public parks, from the municipality. This is only possible with clear and transparent funding. We are not in favor of social mortgages; in our view, the pilot has failed.

113775.explanation: The management agreement for the AZC Parkweg runs until 2028 and will not be extended. After 2028, new shelter will only be provided temporarily for people who have had to leave their homes due to war or persecution.

113776.explanation: People have invested. However, we want to make it clear that if there are reports of nuisance, we will do everything possible to stop it. We also do not see wood burning as sustainable.

113778.explanation: If the national ban is continued - which we oppose - this is a good alternative.

113790.explanation: More weapons for enforcement officers (BOA’s) are not the solution; Belang van Enschede chooses preventive safety through street coaches, private security, and specialized BOA’s.

113791.explanation: Belang van Enschede wants to set up a fund for starting or newly establishing healthcare providers - such as general practitioners, dentists, and other primary care providers.

113795.explanation: Solidarity requires reciprocity. Those who apply for social assistance may expect support but must also show effort to become self-sufficient as soon as possible. However, social work should never lead to displacement in the labor market.

113796.explanation: Financial stability is an important condition for physical and mental well-being. Therefore, we want to focus more on prevention, early detection, and actively reducing debts. The principle is that every resident should have the prospect of a debt-free existence.

113797.explanation: Enschede currently applies a limit of 120%. Belang van Enschede wants to raise this limit to 130% of the social minimum.

113799.explanation: No more parking spaces reserved for target groups and then left unoccupied.

113800.explanation: Our residents must be able to host their guests 'hospitably.' Parking permits aimed at subsequently reducing parking spaces are therefore not seen as a solution.

113801.explanation: Everyone must be able to receive care without restrictions based on their religion, culture, or beliefs. It is not always necessary for a care provider to fully match their preferences.

113802.explanation: This can be done perfectly through the food bank.

113803.explanation: Belang van Enschede wants clarity about the relationship between investments, property tax (OZB) revenues, and inflation adjustments. We cut costs by working more efficiently and making tough decisions internally, not by placing extra pressure on residents.

113806.explanation: Supply and demand must be balanced and aligned with the economic vision. Initially, we must carefully examine the layout of the current sites.

113807.explanation: We want to prioritize affordable housing for residents who demonstrably live or have lived in Enschede.

113808.explanation: We want a re-evaluation of the DIFTAR policy and, for example, not penalizing but rewarding proper waste disposal at designated locations.

113809.explanation: In 2017, the then-majority parties decided that the swimming pool in Glanerbrug must close. We basically support Glanerbrug having a pool, but as of 2026 (after several studies) it has proven financially unfeasible to realize a new swimming facility.

113810.explanation: The content of the lessons is the responsibility of the educational institution.

113812.explanation: Coffeeshops operate in a tense situation: sales are legal, but procurement is not. Belang van Enschede advocates for clear and honest policy. Together with the licensed coffeeshops, we will work actively to combat street dealing, and enforcement will be done without extra burden on the police.

113813.explanation: Belang van Enschede wants to remove obstacles and prioritize affordable housing for residents who demonstrably live or have lived in Enschede. But above all, building according to demand where the role of government (within our frameworks) is mainly to facilitate, not dictate.

113816.explanation: We want neighborhood guides in multiple locations in the city, linked to neighborhood centers (in every district), with neighborhood teams working from there. Neighborhood guides should be easily accessible and open seven days a week.

113818.explanation: To keep the city center lively and socially safe, living in the city center is very important. Residency contributes to supervision, involvement, and safety. Therefore, Belang van Enschede encourages living in the city center, preferably through new and appropriate housing concepts.

113820.explanation: The opening hours are fine. But the shopkeeper decides for themselves and can apply for different opening hours with a permit in case of serious objections.

113822.explanation: We want to recalibrate DIFTAR and: - separate waste at source; - camera surveillance at recycling centers to prevent nuisance; - underground/above-ground placement of containers; - a maximum of three containers per household, with a per-kilo fee; - enforcement and rewards for good behavior, not extra taxes.

113823.explanation: We want to: - preserve the village and neighborhood character; - protect and use the rural area for recreation and controlled temporary housing; - enable new housing concepts under strict rules and monitoring of safety and liveliness; - establish an advisory committee for 'village and rural area.'

113825.explanation: We are open to innovative and commercial developments at Airport Twente.

113768.explanation: It’s about the quality of people. Color, religion, or gender does not matter.

113769.explanation: Every opinion counts. For that reason, we are not a party but a movement where every vote counts.

113770.explanation: Sports clubs or other local initiatives often better know how a sports hall or park can be better integrated into the neighborhood.

113772.explanation: Restaurants and cafés themselves know best when to open to earn their money.

113775.explanation: We currently have such a severe housing shortage in Enschede that we must first dedicate the space, money, and attention to our own citizens instead of an asylum seekers center.

113776.explanation: Gas prices and district heating are almost unaffordable, so these alternatives must remain available.

113778.explanation: Waste of money; it is better used for poverty alleviation.

113790.explanation: BOAs are not police officers and serve the citizens.

113791.explanation: This would promote the flow from social rental housing to owner-occupied homes.

113795.explanation: Tailor-made solutions are needed. Coercion doesn’t work. People want to contribute but it must fit within their limitations. Volunteering costs people work because of wear on clothes and other expenses they incur, which should be compensated.

113796.explanation: Someone on social assistance can barely make ends meet. How are they supposed to pay this?

113797.explanation: The 120% norm is too low; it should be 140%. Also, consideration must be given to the fact that people in owner-occupied homes can also end up in poverty. There must be a safety net for this as well.

113799.explanation: We already have enough of those, and they are rarely used.

113800.explanation: Parking should not become a cash cow for the municipality.

113801.explanation: We believe that people should have a choice in how they want to be cared for, and this should be taken into account.

113802.explanation: Poverty in Enschede is widespread and every little bit helps.

113806.explanation: There is already too much vacancy.

113807.explanation: The housing shortage is too severe, causing increasing resentment towards refugees.

113808.explanation: This would save the municipality of Enschede a lot of money. It prevents illegal dumping of waste.

113809.explanation: Otherwise, there is a high chance that Glanerbrug will have a generation that cannot swim. The swimming pool in Enschede is too far away and people have no transport.

113810.explanation: Swimming lessons have become unaffordable for many residents of Enschede.

113812.explanation: Otherwise, the municipality of Enschede will lose control and oversight of usage in Enschede.

113813.explanation: 25% social rental housing is far too low. The housing shortage is high.

113816.explanation: This creates more connection in the neighborhood, encourages people to do volunteer work, and is good against loneliness.

113818.explanation: There are plenty of parking spaces, only they are unaffordable.

113820.explanation: Shopkeepers should have more freedom to decide when they are open.

113822.explanation: If you make waste disposal cheaper and/or arrange for collection, then you won’t have a waste problem.

113823.explanation: Because we do not know what is meant by nature areas, we are neither for nor against it. It must be looked at case by case.

113825.explanation: Create a new residential neighborhood there.

The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.

The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

In Enschede, the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain allowed.
